---
title: "The Tech Zone"
description: "Tech tutorials, dotfiles, and lessons from building my personal computing stack — Arch Linux, Neovim, tiling and scrolling window managers, and a privacy-first home setup."
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: true
---

```{=html}
<style>
#title-block-header {
  display: none;
}

/* Tech Zone — its own terminal-flavored personality.
   We deliberately diverge from the Research Blog (blue, sans-serif)
   to signal: this is build logs, not literature. */
:root {
  --tz-accent: #22c55e;            /* terminal green */
  --tz-prompt: #38bdf8;             /* sky-cyan for the $ prompt char */
  --tz-warn:   #f59e0b;             /* amber for in-progress / warning */
  --tz-fail:   #ef4444;             /* red for failed-boot card */
  --tz-mono:   "Iosevka", "JetBrains Mono", "Fira Code", ui-monospace,
               SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace;

  /* Page background — a soft slate-cyan that picks up the prompt color
     subtly and complements the terminal-green cards. Reads like a calm
     code-editor canvas, not stark white. */
  --tz-page-bg:     #eef2f5;

  --tz-card-bg:     #eef7ee;
  --tz-card-border: #b6c4ad;
  --tz-card-inner:  #f7fbf6;
  --tz-screen-bg:   #0f172a;        /* near-black terminal background */
  --tz-screen-fg:   #e2e8f0;
  --tz-screen-mute: #94a3b8;
}

body.quarto-dark {
  --tz-accent: #4ade80;
  --tz-prompt: #7dd3fc;
  --tz-warn:   #fbbf24;
  --tz-fail:   #f87171;
  --tz-page-bg:     #0b1220;
  --tz-card-bg:     rgba(31, 41, 55, 0.92);
  --tz-card-border: rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.28);
  --tz-card-inner:  rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.65);
  --tz-screen-bg:   #020617;
  --tz-screen-fg:   #e2e8f0;
  --tz-screen-mute: #94a3b8;
}

/* Extend the page bg across every Quarto wrapper so there's no white
   leakage at the perimeter. The body.quarto-light selectors match
   site-wide rule specificity to win the cascade. */
html, body,
body.quarto-light,
body.quarto-light #quarto-content,
body.quarto-light .footer,
body.quarto-dark,
body.quarto-dark #quarto-content,
body.quarto-dark .footer,
main, main.content, main.column-page,
#quarto-content, #quarto-document-content,
.page-columns, .quarto-page,
#quarto-margin-sidebar,
.column-page, .column-page-left, .column-page-right,
.page-footer, footer.footer, .nav-footer {
  background: var(--tz-page-bg) !important;
}

body.quarto-light #quarto-content,
body.quarto-light .footer,
body.quarto-dark #quarto-content,
body.quarto-dark .footer {
  max-width: none !important;
  margin-left: 0 !important;
  margin-right: 0 !important;
}

.page-footer, footer.footer, .nav-footer { border-top: none !important; }

/* Widen the content column so the header and cards have real room. */
.page-columns {
  grid-template-columns: [screen-start] 4rem [screen-start-inset] 0 [page-start] 0 [page-start-inset] 0 [body-start-outset] 0 [body-start] 0 [body-content-start] 1fr [body-content-end] 0 [body-end] 0 [body-end-outset] 0 [page-end-inset] 0 [page-end] 0 [screen-end-inset] 4rem [screen-end] !important;
}

main.content.column-page {
  padding-left: 0 !important;
  padding-right: 0 !important;
}

.tz-shell {
  max-width: 100%;
  margin: 0 auto;
  padding: 3rem clamp(1rem, 2vw, 2rem) 5rem;
  position: relative;
  z-index: 2;
}

/* ─────────────────────────  TERMINAL HEADER  ───────────────────────── */

.tz-terminal {
  width: 100%;
  max-width: 96rem;
  margin: 0 0 2.6rem;
  border: 1px solid var(--border);
  border-radius: 0.6rem;
  overflow: hidden;
  background: var(--bg-secondary);
  box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.02), 0 12px 30px -20px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.25);
}

.tz-terminal-bar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.45rem;
  padding: 0.55rem 0.85rem;
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--bg-secondary), #000 8%);
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border);
}

.tz-terminal-bar .dot {
  width: 0.7rem; height: 0.7rem; border-radius: 50%; background: #cbd5e1;
}
.tz-terminal-bar .dot.red    { background: #ef4444; }
.tz-terminal-bar .dot.yellow { background: #f59e0b; }
.tz-terminal-bar .dot.green  { background: #22c55e; }

.tz-terminal-bar .title {
  margin-left: 0.6rem;
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.75rem;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}

.tz-terminal-body {
  padding: 1.4rem 1.4rem 1.6rem;
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
}

.tz-prompt-line {
  margin: 0 0 0.4rem;
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  letter-spacing: 0.01em;
}

.tz-prompt-line .user { color: var(--tz-accent); font-weight: 700; }
.tz-prompt-line .sep  { color: var(--text-secondary); }
.tz-prompt-line .path { color: var(--tz-prompt); font-weight: 600; }
.tz-prompt-line .glyph { color: var(--text-secondary); margin: 0 0.35rem; }

.tz-title {
  margin: 0.3rem 0 0.7rem;
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: clamp(1.85rem, 3.4vw, 2.6rem);
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: -0.01em;
  line-height: 1.1;
}

.tz-title .cursor {
  display: inline-block;
  width: 0.55ch;
  margin-left: 0.15ch;
  background: var(--tz-accent);
  color: transparent;
  animation: tz-blink 1.05s steps(2, end) infinite;
}

@keyframes tz-blink { 50% { background: transparent; } }

.tz-intro {
  max-width: 68rem;
  margin: 0 0 0.65rem;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  font-size: 0.97rem;
  line-height: 1.75;
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
}
.tz-intro:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }

.tz-intro .label {
  color: var(--tz-accent);
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}

.tz-intro code {
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-accent), transparent 86%);
  border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-accent), transparent 70%);
  color: var(--tz-accent);
  padding: 0.02em 0.35em;
  border-radius: 0.25rem;
  font-size: 0.92em;
}

/* ─────────────────────────  FILTER STRIP  ───────────────────────── */

.tz-filter-strip {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.85rem;
  margin: 0 0 1.6rem;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
}

.tz-filter-input {
  flex: 1 1 22rem;
  min-width: 14rem;
  max-width: 32rem;
  padding: 0.55rem 0.85rem 0.55rem 2.2rem;
  border: 1.5px solid var(--tz-card-border);
  border-radius: 0.7rem;
  background:
    color-mix(in oklab, var(--bg-secondary), transparent 30%)
    url("data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 24 24' fill='none' stroke='%2394a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'><circle cx='11' cy='11' r='7'/><line x1='21' y1='21' x2='16.65' y2='16.65'/></svg>") no-repeat 0.65rem center / 1rem 1rem;
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  color: inherit;
  transition: border-color 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease;
}

.tz-filter-input:focus {
  outline: none;
  border-color: var(--tz-accent);
  box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--tz-accent) 22%, transparent);
}

.tz-filter-count {
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.78rem;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
}
.tz-filter-count strong { color: var(--tz-accent); font-weight: 700; }

/* ─────────────────────────  CARD GRID  ───────────────────────── */

.tz-section-kicker {
  margin: 0 0 0.5rem;
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.78rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.14em;
  color: var(--tz-accent);
}

.tz-section-kicker::before { content: "// "; opacity: 0.55; }

.tz-section-title {
  margin: 0 0 1.4rem;
  font-size: clamp(1.4rem, 2.4vw, 1.8rem);
  font-weight: 700;
  line-height: 1.15;
}

.tz-card-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
  gap: 1.5rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}

.tz-card {
  position: relative;
  display: block;
  border: 1.5px solid var(--tz-card-border);
  border-radius: 1.35rem;
  background: var(--tz-card-bg);
  overflow: hidden;
  text-decoration: none;
  color: inherit;
  transition: border-color 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease, transform 0.2s ease;
}

.tz-card:hover,
.tz-card:focus-visible {
  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--tz-accent) 38%, var(--tz-card-border));
  box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.10);
  transform: translateY(-2px);
  text-decoration: none;
  color: inherit;
}

.tz-card:focus-visible {
  outline: 2px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--tz-accent) 55%, transparent);
  outline-offset: 0.2rem;
}

.tz-card.failed:hover,
.tz-card.failed:focus-visible {
  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--tz-fail) 38%, var(--tz-card-border));
}

.tz-card[hidden] { display: none; }

.tz-card-summary {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  gap: 1.1rem;
  padding: 1.55rem;
}

.tz-card-tags {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.5rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.6rem;
}

.tz-tag {
  padding: 0.28rem 0.7rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  border: 1.2px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--tz-accent) 35%, var(--tz-card-border));
  color: var(--tz-accent);
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-accent), transparent 90%);
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.74rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}

.tz-tag::before { content: "#"; opacity: 0.6; margin-right: 0.05em; }

.tz-card.failed .tz-tag {
  color: var(--tz-fail);
  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--tz-fail) 35%, var(--tz-card-border));
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-fail), transparent 90%);
}

.tz-card-name-row {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.85rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.45rem;
}

.tz-card-number {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  width: 2rem; height: 2rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-accent), transparent 82%);
  color: var(--tz-accent);
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  font-weight: 800;
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}

.tz-card.failed .tz-card-number {
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-fail), transparent 82%);
  color: var(--tz-fail);
}

.tz-card-name {
  margin: 0;
  font-size: 1.22rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  line-height: 1.25;
}

.tz-card-meta {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.75rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.74rem;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}

.tz-card-date::before { content: "[ "; opacity: 0.55; }
.tz-card-date::after  { content: " ]"; opacity: 0.55; }

.tz-status-pill {
  padding: 0.18rem 0.55rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.05em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  border: 1px solid currentColor;
}

.tz-status-pill.ok    { color: var(--tz-accent); background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-accent), transparent 92%); }
.tz-status-pill.warn  { color: var(--tz-warn);   background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-warn),   transparent 92%); }
.tz-status-pill.fail  { color: var(--tz-fail);   background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-fail),   transparent 92%); }

.tz-card-preview-copy {
  margin: 0;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  line-height: 1.7;
}

.tz-card-cta {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.4rem;
  margin-top: 0.5rem;
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: var(--tz-accent);
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
  transition: gap 0.18s ease;
}

.tz-card.failed .tz-card-cta { color: var(--tz-fail); }

.tz-card:hover .tz-card-cta,
.tz-card:focus-visible .tz-card-cta { gap: 0.7rem; }

.tz-card-cta .arrow { transition: transform 0.18s ease; }
.tz-card:hover .tz-card-cta .arrow,
.tz-card:focus-visible .tz-card-cta .arrow { transform: translateX(2px); }

/* ──── The "screen" preview inside each card — a mock terminal session ──── */

.tz-screen {
  background: var(--tz-screen-bg);
  border-radius: 0.75rem;
  border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--tz-screen-bg) 50%, var(--tz-card-border));
  overflow: hidden;
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  line-height: 1.55;
  color: var(--tz-screen-fg);
  position: relative;
}

.tz-screen-bar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.4rem;
  padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.04);
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08);
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
  color: var(--tz-screen-mute);
}

.tz-screen-bar .dot {
  width: 0.55rem; height: 0.55rem; border-radius: 50%; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.18);
}
.tz-screen-bar .dot.red    { background: #ef4444; }
.tz-screen-bar .dot.yellow { background: #f59e0b; }
.tz-screen-bar .dot.green  { background: #22c55e; }

.tz-screen-bar .label {
  margin-left: 0.3rem;
  flex: 1;
}

.tz-screen-bar .badge {
  padding: 0.05rem 0.45rem;
  border-radius: 0.3rem;
  font-size: 0.65rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
}

.tz-screen-bar .badge.ok   { color: var(--tz-accent); background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-accent), transparent 82%); }
.tz-screen-bar .badge.fail { color: var(--tz-fail);   background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-fail),   transparent 82%); }

.tz-screen-body {
  padding: 0.95rem 1.05rem 1.1rem;
  white-space: pre;
  overflow-x: auto;
}

.tz-screen .p   { color: var(--tz-screen-mute); }
.tz-screen .u   { color: var(--tz-accent); font-weight: 600; }
.tz-screen .c   { color: var(--tz-prompt); }
.tz-screen .ok  { color: var(--tz-accent); }
.tz-screen .err { color: var(--tz-fail); font-weight: 600; }
.tz-screen .w   { color: var(--tz-warn); }
.tz-screen .mute { color: var(--tz-screen-mute); }

/* ─────────────────────────  PLANNED / UPCOMING  ─────────────────────────
   These cards represent articles I've committed to writing but haven't
   shipped yet. Visually distinct so visitors don't click expecting
   content: amber accent, dashed border, no hover-lift, no link.       */

.tz-divider {
  margin: 3.2rem 0 1.6rem;
  border-top: 1px dashed var(--tz-card-border);
}

.tz-section-kicker.warn { color: var(--tz-warn); }
.tz-section-kicker.warn::before { color: var(--tz-warn); }

.tz-section-blurb {
  max-width: 56rem;
  margin: -0.6rem 0 1.6rem;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.88rem;
  line-height: 1.7;
}

.tz-card.planned {
  cursor: default;
  border-style: dashed;
  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--tz-warn) 40%, var(--tz-card-border));
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-warn), var(--tz-card-bg) 94%);
}

.tz-card.planned:hover,
.tz-card.planned:focus-visible {
  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--tz-warn) 40%, var(--tz-card-border));
  box-shadow: none;
  transform: none;
}

.tz-card.planned .tz-card-number {
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-warn), transparent 82%);
  color: var(--tz-warn);
}

.tz-card.planned .tz-tag {
  color: var(--tz-warn);
  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--tz-warn) 35%, var(--tz-card-border));
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-warn), transparent 90%);
}

.tz-card.planned .tz-status-pill.warn { background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-warn), transparent 86%); }

.tz-planned-scope {
  margin: 0.2rem 0 0;
  padding: 0.85rem 1rem;
  font-family: var(--tz-mono);
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  line-height: 1.7;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--tz-warn), transparent 93%);
  border: 1px dashed color-mix(in srgb, var(--tz-warn) 30%, var(--tz-card-border));
  border-radius: 0.6rem;
}

.tz-planned-scope .label {
  display: inline-block;
  color: var(--tz-warn);
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  margin-right: 0.45rem;
}

.tz-planned-scope ul {
  margin: 0.45rem 0 0;
  padding-left: 1.1rem;
}

.tz-planned-scope li { margin-bottom: 0.15rem; }
.tz-planned-scope li:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }

/* ─────────────────────────  RESPONSIVE  ───────────────────────── */

@media (max-width: 1100px) {
  .tz-card-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
}

@media (max-width: 640px) {
  .page-columns {
    grid-template-columns: [screen-start] 0.5rem [screen-start-inset] 0 [page-start] 0 [page-start-inset] 0 [body-start-outset] 0 [body-start] 0 [body-content-start] 1fr [body-content-end] 0 [body-end] 0 [body-end-outset] 0 [page-end-inset] 0 [page-end] 0 [screen-end-inset] 0.5rem [screen-end] !important;
  }
  .tz-shell { padding: 2.5rem 0.75rem 4rem; }
  .tz-terminal-body { padding: 1.1rem 1rem 1.3rem; }
  .tz-card-summary  { padding: 1.25rem; }
  .tz-screen-body   { font-size: 0.74rem; }
}
</style>

<div class="tz-shell">

  <!-- ─────── Page lead-in (above the terminal header) ─────── -->
  <h2 class="tz-section-title">Tech Articles and Tutorials.</h2>

  <!-- ─────── Terminal-styled section header ─────── -->
  <div class="tz-terminal" role="img" aria-label="Terminal window header for The Tech Zone">
    <div class="tz-terminal-bar">
      <span class="dot red"></span>
      <span class="dot yellow"></span>
      <span class="dot green"></span>
      <span class="title">evannsmc@tech-zone: ~</span>
    </div>
    <div class="tz-terminal-body">
      <p class="tz-prompt-line">
        <span class="user">evannsmc</span><span class="sep">@</span><span class="user">arch</span><span class="glyph">:</span><span class="path">~/tech-zone</span><span class="glyph">$</span> cat README.md
      </p>
      <h1 class="tz-title">The Tech Zone<span class="cursor">_</span></h1>
      <p class="tz-intro">
        Tech tutorials, dotfiles, and lessons from building my personal computing stack piece by piece.
      </p>
      <p class="tz-intro">
        <span class="label">Currently:</span> my first <code>Arch Linux</code> install on an old laptop —
        on the way to a dual-boot Ubuntu/Arch workstation with <code>BTRFS</code>, <code>LUKS</code>,
        and <code>zram</code>, and eventually a triple-boot with a minimal Windows partition for
        collaborators who live in Office-suite land.
      </p>
      <p class="tz-intro">
        <span class="label">Next on this thread:</span> <code>Neovim</code> from scratch, tiling and scrolling
        window managers, and whatever else I break and fix.
      </p>
      <p class="tz-intro">
        <span class="label">Eventually:</span> a privacy-first home setup with custom DNS routing,
        a personal VPN I can connect to from anywhere, and self-hosted services on a NAS.
      </p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- ─────── Filter strip ─────── -->
  <div class="tz-filter-strip">
    <input
      class="tz-filter-input"
      type="search"
      placeholder="filter by title, topic, or #tag…"
      aria-label="Filter articles"
      autocomplete="off"
    />
    <span class="tz-filter-count" aria-live="polite">
      showing <strong id="tz-count-shown">10</strong> of <strong>10</strong>
    </span>
  </div>

  <!-- ─────── Published / live articles ─────── -->
  <p class="tz-section-kicker">published</p>

  <!-- ─────── Card grid ─────── -->
  <div class="tz-card-grid" id="tz-card-grid">

    <!-- CARD 01 — Basic Arch install (success) -->
    <a class="tz-card success" href="./posts/2026-05-01-first-arch-install/"
       data-tags="arch linux first install ext4 grub systemd basic boot"
       aria-label="Read: My First Arch Linux Install — A Basic Setup on an Old Laptop">
      <div class="tz-card-summary">
        <div class="tz-card-tags">
          <span class="tz-tag">arch linux</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">first install</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">ext4</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">grub</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">systemd</span>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-name-row">
          <span class="tz-card-number">01</span>
          <h3 class="tz-card-name">My First Arch Linux Install: A Basic Setup on an Old Laptop</h3>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-meta">
          <span class="tz-card-date">2026-05-01</span>
          <span class="tz-status-pill ok">boot ok</span>
        </div>

        <p class="tz-card-preview-copy">
          A copy-pasteable walkthrough of installing Arch Linux 2026.05.01 on an old test laptop — verifying
          the ISO, partitioning, pacstrapping, configuring locale and users, and getting GRUB to actually boot
          the thing. No Btrfs, no LUKS, no zram — just the boring, dependable first install.
        </p>

        <div class="tz-screen" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="tz-screen-bar">
            <span class="dot red"></span><span class="dot yellow"></span><span class="dot green"></span>
            <span class="label">post-install — boot check</span>
            <span class="badge ok">boot ok</span>
          </div>
          <div class="tz-screen-body"><span class="p">[evannsmc@arch ~]$</span> <span class="c">lsblk</span>
NAME        SIZE  TYPE  MOUNTPOINTS
nvme0n1     465G  disk
├─nvme0n1p1   1G  part  /boot
├─nvme0n1p2  35G  part  <span class="mute">[SWAP]</span>
├─nvme0n1p3 200G  part  /
└─nvme0n1p4 229G  part  /home

<span class="p">[evannsmc@arch ~]$</span> <span class="c">systemctl is-system-running</span>
<span class="ok">running</span>

<span class="p">[evannsmc@arch ~]$</span> <span class="c">uname -r</span>
6.13.4-arch1-1<span class="u">_</span></div>
        </div>

        <span class="tz-card-cta">read the install log <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

    <!-- CARD 02 — Ambitious LUKS+Btrfs install (failed) -->
    <a class="tz-card failed" href="./posts/2026-05-05-arch-luks-btrfs-lessons/"
       data-tags="arch linux luks btrfs mkinitcpio debugging boot failure dual-boot ubuntu zram"
       aria-label="Read: An Ambitious Arch Install That Didn't Boot — LUKS, Btrfs, and What I Learned">
      <div class="tz-card-summary">
        <div class="tz-card-tags">
          <span class="tz-tag">arch linux</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">luks</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">btrfs</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">mkinitcpio</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">debugging</span>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-name-row">
          <span class="tz-card-number">02</span>
          <h3 class="tz-card-name">An Ambitious Arch Install That Didn't Boot — LUKS, Btrfs & Lessons</h3>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-meta">
          <span class="tz-card-date">2026-05-05</span>
          <span class="tz-status-pill fail">in progress</span>
        </div>

        <p class="tz-card-preview-copy">
          The opposite of the boring install: full-disk LUKS, Btrfs subvolumes for <code>/</code> and
          <code>/home</code>, zram swap, a shared data partition, and dual-boot with Ubuntu. GRUB loaded,
          the LUKS prompt accepted my passphrase, and then the kernel dropped me into emergency mode.
          The plan, the failure, and everything I've tried so far.
        </p>

        <div class="tz-screen" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="tz-screen-bar">
            <span class="dot red"></span><span class="dot yellow"></span><span class="dot green"></span>
            <span class="label">boot — emergency.target</span>
            <span class="badge fail">boot failed</span>
          </div>
          <div class="tz-screen-body"><span class="mute">A start job is running for /dev/disk/by-uuid/0e6caf44-…</span>
<span class="mute">A password is required to access the Arch volume.</span>

<span class="err">[FAIL]</span> <span class="mute">Timed out waiting for device</span>
        <span class="mute">/dev/disk/by-uuid/0e6caf44-...</span>
<span class="err">[DEPEND]</span> <span class="mute">Dependency failed for /sysroot.</span>
<span class="err">[DEPEND]</span> <span class="mute">Dependency failed for initrd-root-fs.target.</span>

<span class="w">You are in emergency mode.</span>

<span class="p">root@archiso ~ #</span> <span class="c">ls /dev/mapper</span>
control  arch  <span class="mute">…</span><span class="u">_</span></div>
        </div>

        <span class="tz-card-cta">read the post-mortem <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

    <!-- CARD 03 — LUKS + BTRFS + Caelestia (working) -->
    <a class="tz-card success" href="./posts/2026-05-17-arch-luks-btrfs-caelestia/"
       data-tags="arch linux luks btrfs caelestia hyprland snapper hibernation zram sd-encrypt grub pipewire"
       aria-label="Read: Arch + LUKS + Btrfs + Caelestia — The Install I'm Actually Using">
      <div class="tz-card-summary">
        <div class="tz-card-tags">
          <span class="tz-tag">arch linux</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">luks</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">btrfs</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">caelestia</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">hyprland</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">snapper</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">hibernation</span>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-name-row">
          <span class="tz-card-number">03</span>
          <h3 class="tz-card-name">Arch + LUKS + Btrfs + Caelestia — The Install I'm Actually Using</h3>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-meta">
          <span class="tz-card-date">2026-05-17</span>
          <span class="tz-status-pill ok">boot ok</span>
        </div>

        <p class="tz-card-preview-copy">
          Article 02's ambition, executed correctly: <code>LUKS2</code> on the root partition,
          <code>Btrfs</code> with the canonical <code>@</code>/<code>@home</code>/<code>@snapshots</code>/<code>@var_log</code>/<code>@swap</code>
          subvolumes, zram for everyday swap plus a NoCOW Btrfs swap file for hibernation,
          snapper + grub-btrfs from day one, and the Caelestia Hyprland desktop on top —
          with every <code>cryptdevice=</code> typo from the failed-boot post-mortem fixed.
        </p>

        <div class="tz-screen" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="tz-screen-bar">
            <span class="dot red"></span><span class="dot yellow"></span><span class="dot green"></span>
            <span class="label">first boot — sanity checks</span>
            <span class="badge ok">boot ok</span>
          </div>
          <div class="tz-screen-body"><span class="p">[evanns@arch ~]$</span> <span class="c">cryptsetup status cryptroot</span>
<span class="mute">  type:    LUKS2</span>
<span class="mute">  cipher:  aes-xts-plain64</span>
<span class="mute">  device:  /dev/nvme0n1p2</span>

<span class="p">[evanns@arch ~]$</span> <span class="c">findmnt -t btrfs -o TARGET,OPTIONS</span>
TARGET       OPTIONS
/            <span class="ok">subvol=/@</span>,compress=zstd:3,noatime
/home        <span class="ok">subvol=/@home</span>,compress=zstd:3
/.snapshots  <span class="ok">subvol=/@snapshots</span>,compress=zstd:3
/var/log     <span class="ok">subvol=/@var_log</span>,compress=zstd:3
/swap        <span class="ok">subvol=/@swap</span>,noatime

<span class="p">[evanns@arch ~]$</span> <span class="c">swapon --show</span>
NAME           TYPE       SIZE PRIO
/dev/zram0     partition  8G   <span class="ok">100</span>
/swap/swapfile file      20G   <span class="mute">-2</span>

<span class="p">[evanns@arch ~]$</span> <span class="c">hyprctl version | head -1</span>
Hyprland, built from branch main<span class="u">_</span></div>
        </div>

        <span class="tz-card-cta">read the build log <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

    <!-- CARD 04 — Pacman / Yay / AUR reference -->
    <a class="tz-card success" href="./posts/2026-05-17-pacman-yay-aur-prompts/"
       data-tags="arch linux pacman aur yay package management reference prompts hooks snap-pac mkinitcpio stow"
       aria-label="Read: Pacman, Yay, and the AUR — What Every Prompt Actually Means">
      <div class="tz-card-summary">
        <div class="tz-card-tags">
          <span class="tz-tag">arch linux</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">pacman</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">aur</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">yay</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">reference</span>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-name-row">
          <span class="tz-card-number">04</span>
          <h3 class="tz-card-name">Pacman, Yay, and the AUR — What Every Prompt Actually Means</h3>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-meta">
          <span class="tz-card-date">2026-05-17</span>
          <span class="tz-status-pill ok">reference</span>
        </div>

        <p class="tz-card-preview-copy">
          A practical reference for every interactive prompt, warning, and error you'll see during pacman
          installs and AUR builds — <code>Y/n</code> confirmations, provider selection, file conflicts,
          <code>cleanBuild</code> / diffs / PKGBUILDs-to-edit, the snap-pac perl warning that looks scary
          but isn't, and how to layer your own dotfiles on top of opinionated bundles with GNU Stow.
        </p>

        <div class="tz-screen" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="tz-screen-bar">
            <span class="dot red"></span><span class="dot yellow"></span><span class="dot green"></span>
            <span class="label">yay — interactive prompts</span>
            <span class="badge ok">reference</span>
          </div>
          <div class="tz-screen-body"><span class="p">[evanns@arch ~]$</span> <span class="c">yay -S caelestia-meta</span>
  3 caelestia-shell        <span class="mute">(Build Files Exist)</span>
  2 quickshell-git         <span class="mute">(Build Files Exist)</span>
  1 libcava                <span class="mute">(Build Files Exist)</span>
<span class="ok">==></span> Packages to cleanBuild?
<span class="ok">==></span> [N]one [<span class="u">A</span>]ll [Ab]ort [I]nstalled [No]tInstalled

<span class="ok">==></span> Diffs to show?
<span class="ok">==></span> [<span class="u">N</span>]one [A]ll [Ab]ort [I]nstalled [No]tInstalled

<span class="ok">==></span> PKGBUILDs to edit?
<span class="ok">==></span> [<span class="u">N</span>]one [A]ll [Ab]ort [I]nstalled [No]tInstalled<span class="u">_</span></div>
        </div>

        <span class="tz-card-cta">read the reference <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

  </div>

  <!-- ─────── Upcoming / planned articles ─────── -->
  <div class="tz-divider" role="separator" aria-hidden="true"></div>

  <p class="tz-section-kicker warn">upcoming · drafts in progress</p>
  <h3 class="tz-section-title" id="tz-upcoming">Planned Articles</h3>
  <p class="tz-section-blurb">
    The roadmap below is a queue, not a library — these are write-ups I've committed to but haven't shipped yet,
    listed in the order I plan to publish them. Cards are dashed, amber-flagged, and intentionally non-clickable
    so it's clear there's nothing to read yet. Check back, or watch the commit log if you're impatient.
  </p>

  <div class="tz-card-grid" id="tz-planned-grid">

    <!-- CARD 03 — 2019 Mac mini Arch install (special Wi-Fi) -->
    <div class="tz-card planned" role="article"
         data-tags="arch linux mac mini 2019 broadcom wifi bcm4360 broadcom-wl firmware basic ext4 grub"
         aria-labelledby="tz-planned-03">
      <div class="tz-card-summary">
        <div class="tz-card-tags">
          <span class="tz-tag">arch linux</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">2019 mac mini</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">broadcom wifi</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">bcm4360</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">ext4</span>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-name-row">
          <span class="tz-card-number">03</span>
          <h3 class="tz-card-name" id="tz-planned-03">Arch on a 2019 Mac mini — Same Basic Install, Special Wi-Fi Card</h3>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-meta">
          <span class="tz-card-date">tba</span>
          <span class="tz-status-pill warn">planned</span>
        </div>

        <p class="tz-card-preview-copy">
          The boring-but-dependable first install, ported to a 2019 Mac mini. The catch: its Broadcom
          Wi-Fi card isn't supported by the default kernel modules, so the install needs an Ethernet
          bootstrap and the proprietary <code>broadcom-wl</code> driver before networking comes back.
        </p>

        <div class="tz-planned-scope">
          <span class="label">scope</span>
          <ul>
            <li>Mirrors Article 01 (ext4, GRUB, systemd, no LUKS, no Btrfs)</li>
            <li>Diverges on Wi-Fi: <code>broadcom-wl</code> via wired install + DKMS</li>
            <li>Notes on Apple T2 / EFI quirks specific to this Mac mini revision</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- CARD 04 — Arch + LUKS only -->
    <div class="tz-card planned" role="article"
         data-tags="arch linux luks encryption full disk encryption ext4 cryptsetup mkinitcpio"
         aria-labelledby="tz-planned-04">
      <div class="tz-card-summary">
        <div class="tz-card-tags">
          <span class="tz-tag">arch linux</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">luks</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">encryption</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">ext4</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">cryptsetup</span>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-name-row">
          <span class="tz-card-number">04</span>
          <h3 class="tz-card-name" id="tz-planned-04">Arch + LUKS — One Step Up: Add Encryption, Change Nothing Else</h3>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-meta">
          <span class="tz-card-date">tba</span>
          <span class="tz-status-pill warn">planned</span>
        </div>

        <p class="tz-card-preview-copy">
          The minimum diff from Article 01: same ext4 root, same GRUB, same partitioning — but the
          root device is now a LUKS container that asks for a passphrase at boot. The goal is to
          isolate the encryption variable from the filesystem variable.
        </p>

        <div class="tz-planned-scope">
          <span class="label">scope</span>
          <ul>
            <li>Single-disk LUKS1/LUKS2 on the root partition, unencrypted <code>/boot</code></li>
            <li><code>encrypt</code> hook in <code>mkinitcpio.conf</code>, kernel cmdline via GRUB</li>
            <li>No Btrfs, no subvolumes, no zram — pure encryption-only delta</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- CARD 05 — Arch + BTRFS only -->
    <div class="tz-card planned" role="article"
         data-tags="arch linux btrfs subvolumes snapper snapshots filesystem"
         aria-labelledby="tz-planned-05">
      <div class="tz-card-summary">
        <div class="tz-card-tags">
          <span class="tz-tag">arch linux</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">btrfs</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">subvolumes</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">snapper</span>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-name-row">
          <span class="tz-card-number">05</span>
          <h3 class="tz-card-name" id="tz-planned-05">Arch + Btrfs — Same as Article 01, But With Subvolumes</h3>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-meta">
          <span class="tz-card-date">tba</span>
          <span class="tz-status-pill warn">planned</span>
        </div>

        <p class="tz-card-preview-copy">
          The mirror of Article 04, swapping the variable: plain unencrypted disk, but Btrfs with
          proper subvolumes for <code>/</code>, <code>/home</code>, and <code>/.snapshots</code>, plus
          snapper integration so rollbacks are a real option. Isolates filesystem from encryption.
        </p>

        <div class="tz-planned-scope">
          <span class="label">scope</span>
          <ul>
            <li>Btrfs subvolume layout: <code>@</code>, <code>@home</code>, <code>@snapshots</code>, <code>@log</code></li>
            <li>snapper + <code>grub-btrfs</code> for bootable snapshots</li>
            <li>No LUKS — clear-text disk, so the bootloader story stays simple</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- CARD 06 — Arch + LUKS + BTRFS (single-boot) -->
    <div class="tz-card planned" role="article"
         data-tags="arch linux luks btrfs encryption subvolumes snapper zram single boot"
         aria-labelledby="tz-planned-06">
      <div class="tz-card-summary">
        <div class="tz-card-tags">
          <span class="tz-tag">arch linux</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">luks</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">btrfs</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">snapper</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">zram</span>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-name-row">
          <span class="tz-card-number">06</span>
          <h3 class="tz-card-name" id="tz-planned-06">Arch + LUKS + Btrfs — The Encrypted, Snapshot-Ready Single-Boot</h3>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-meta">
          <span class="tz-card-date">tba</span>
          <span class="tz-status-pill warn">planned</span>
        </div>

        <p class="tz-card-preview-copy">
          The combined version of Articles 04 and 05: LUKS underneath, Btrfs subvolumes on top,
          zram for swap, snapper for rollbacks. Functionally the install Article 02 was reaching for —
          but kept as a single-boot so the bootloader and initramfs only have one OS to wake up.
        </p>

        <div class="tz-planned-scope">
          <span class="label">scope</span>
          <ul>
            <li>LUKS container → Btrfs → subvolumes (no LVM in between)</li>
            <li>zram swap; no separate swap partition</li>
            <li>Single-boot only — multi-boot lives in Articles 07 &amp; 08</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- CARD 07 — Dual-boot Arch + Ubuntu, shared partition -->
    <div class="tz-card planned" role="article"
         data-tags="arch linux ubuntu dual boot luks btrfs shared partition grub os-prober"
         aria-labelledby="tz-planned-07">
      <div class="tz-card-summary">
        <div class="tz-card-tags">
          <span class="tz-tag">arch linux</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">ubuntu</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">dual boot</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">shared partition</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">grub</span>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-name-row">
          <span class="tz-card-number">07</span>
          <h3 class="tz-card-name" id="tz-planned-07">Dual-Boot Arch + Ubuntu — With a Shared Data Partition</h3>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-meta">
          <span class="tz-card-date">tba</span>
          <span class="tz-status-pill warn">planned</span>
        </div>

        <p class="tz-card-preview-copy">
          Everything from Article 06, then add an Ubuntu install alongside it and a shared partition
          both distros can read and write so the documents I actually need on both systems live in
          exactly one place — not synced, not copied, just mounted twice.
        </p>

        <div class="tz-planned-scope">
          <span class="label">scope</span>
          <ul>
            <li>Two encrypted root partitions (Arch + Ubuntu), one EFI System Partition</li>
            <li>Shared data partition (ext4 or exFAT) auto-mounted from both distros</li>
            <li>GRUB <code>os-prober</code> chainload; UUIDs pinned in both fstabs</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- CARD 08 — Triple-boot Arch + Ubuntu + Windows -->
    <div class="tz-card planned" role="article"
         data-tags="arch linux ubuntu windows triple boot luks btrfs shared partition efi bitlocker culmination"
         aria-labelledby="tz-planned-08">
      <div class="tz-card-summary">
        <div class="tz-card-tags">
          <span class="tz-tag">arch linux</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">ubuntu</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">windows</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">triple boot</span>
          <span class="tz-tag">culmination</span>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-name-row">
          <span class="tz-card-number">08</span>
          <h3 class="tz-card-name" id="tz-planned-08">Triple-Boot Arch + Ubuntu + Windows — Closing the Loop on Article 02</h3>
        </div>

        <div class="tz-card-meta">
          <span class="tz-card-date">tba</span>
          <span class="tz-status-pill warn">planned</span>
        </div>

        <p class="tz-card-preview-copy">
          The full-circle finale: every prior article stacked, plus a minimal Windows partition for
          collaborators who live in Office-suite land. The same target that broke the second install —
          but reached on purpose this time, with every lesson from Articles 01–07 underneath.
        </p>

        <div class="tz-planned-scope">
          <span class="label">scope</span>
          <ul>
            <li>Windows installed first (it owns the EFI; everyone else adapts)</li>
            <li>Arch + Ubuntu with shared LUKS/Btrfs layout from Articles 06–07</li>
            <li>Shared data partition readable from Windows, Arch, and Ubuntu</li>
            <li>Retrospective: which choices from Article 02 finally paid off</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>

</div>

<script>
// Live filter for Tech Zone cards.
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
  var input = document.querySelector('.tz-filter-input');
  var count = document.getElementById('tz-count-shown');
  if (!input || !count) return;

  var cards = Array.prototype.slice.call(
    document.querySelectorAll('#tz-card-grid .tz-card, #tz-planned-grid .tz-card')
  );

  function applyFilter() {
    var q = input.value.trim().toLowerCase().replace(/^#/, '');
    var shown = 0;
    cards.forEach(function (card) {
      if (!q) { card.hidden = false; shown++; return; }
      var title = (card.querySelector('.tz-card-name') || {}).textContent || '';
      var tags  = card.getAttribute('data-tags') || '';
      var copy  = (card.querySelector('.tz-card-preview-copy') || {}).textContent || '';
      var hay = (title + ' ' + tags + ' ' + copy).toLowerCase();
      var match = hay.indexOf(q) !== -1;
      card.hidden = !match;
      if (match) shown++;
    });
    count.textContent = String(shown);
  }

  input.addEventListener('input', applyFilter);
});
</script>
```
